revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''観音院'''（かんのんいん）は、[[長野県]][[岡谷市]]にある[[真言宗]][[智山派]]の寺院。山号は龍光山。本尊は[[十一面観...'
revision.text:
'''観音院'''（かんのんいん）は、[[長野県]][[岡谷市]]にある[[真言宗]][[智山派]]の寺院。山号は龍光山。本尊は[[十一面観音]]。 一般的には「小坂観音院」の名で知られる。
==由緒== かつては諏訪大社の社坊であった。中世に諏訪氏の祈願所となった。諏訪湖を見渡せる眺望の良い高台にある。 井上靖の著書「[[風林火山 (小説)|風林火山]]」では、諏訪頼重の娘由布姫は、信玄の側室となって 勝頼を生み、この観音院で暮らす設定になっている。井上靖の小説を原作とした[[2007年]]の[[日本放送協会|ＮＨＫ]][[大河ドラマ]]「[[風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)|風林火山]]」でも観音院が登場する。 昭和38年（[[1963年]]）境内の高台に、[[武田信玄]]の側室[[諏訪御料人]]の供養塔が建てられた。
==外部リンク== *[[http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ 岡谷市役所（商業観光課）ホームページ「由布姫のふるさと 小坂観音院（龍光山観音院）」]
[[category:長野県の寺|かんのんいん]] [[category:岡谷市|かんのんいん]] {{buddhism-stub}}

'''観音院'''（かんのんいん）は、[[長野県]][[岡谷市]]にある[[真言宗]][[智山派]]の寺院。山号は龍光山。本尊は[[十一面観音]]。 一般的には「小坂観音院」の名で知られる。
==由緒== かつては諏訪大社の社坊であった。中世に諏訪氏の祈願所となった。諏訪湖を見渡せる眺望の良い高台にある。 井上靖の著書「[[風林火山 (小説)|風林火山]]」では、諏訪頼重の娘由布姫は、信玄の側室となって 勝頼を生み、この観音院で暮らす設定になっている。井上靖の小説を原作とした[[2007年]]の[[日本放送協会|ＮＨＫ]][[大河ドラマ]]「[[風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)|風林火山]]」でも観音院が登場する。 昭和38年（[[1963年]]）境内の高台に、[[武田信玄]]の側室[[諏訪御料人]]の供養塔が建てられた。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ 岡谷市役所（商業観光課）ホームページ「由布姫のふるさと 小坂観音院（龍光山観音院）」]
[[category:長野県の寺|かんのんいん]] [[category:岡谷市|かんのんいん]] {{buddhism-stub}}

'''観音院'''（かんのんいん）は、[[長野県]][[岡谷市]]にある[[真言宗]][[智山派]]の寺院。山号は龍光山。本尊は[[十一面観音]]。 一般的には「小坂観音院」の名で知られる。
==由緒== かつては諏訪大社の社坊であった。中世に諏訪氏の祈願所となった。諏訪湖を見渡せる眺望の良い高台にある。 井上靖の著書「[[風林火山 (小説)|風林火山]]」では、諏訪頼重の娘由布姫は、信玄の側室となって勝頼を生み、この観音院で暮らす設定になっている。井上靖の小説を原作とした[[平成]]19年（[[2007年]]）の[[日本放送協会|ＮＨＫ]][[大河ドラマ]]「[[風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)|風林火山]]」でも観音院が登場する。 [[昭和]]38年（[[1963年]]）境内の高台に、[[武田信玄]]の側室[[諏訪御料人]]の供養塔が建てられた。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ 岡谷市役所（商業観光課）ホームページ「由布姫のふるさと 小坂観音院（龍光山観音院）」]

[[昭和]]38年（[[1963年]]）境内の高台に、諏訪御料人の供養塔が建てられた。

'''観音院'''（かんのんいん）は、[[長野県]][[岡谷市]]にある[[真言宗智山派]]の寺院。山号は龍光山。本尊は[[十一面観音]]。 一般的には「'''小坂観音院'''」の名で知られる。
==由緒== かつては[[諏訪大社]]の社坊であった。中世に[[諏訪氏]]の祈願所となった。[[諏訪湖]]を見渡せる眺望の良い高台にある。
[[井上靖]]の著書「[[風林火山 (小説)|風林火山]]」では、[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]の娘[[諏訪御料人|由布姫]]（諏訪御料人）は、[[武田信玄]]の側室となって[[武田勝頼]]を生み、この観音院で暮らす設定になっている。井上靖の小説を原作とした[[平成]]19年（[[2007年]]）の[[日本放送協会|NHK]][[大河ドラマ]]「[[風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)|風林火山]]」でも観音院が登場する。
[[昭和]]38年（[[1963年]]）境内の高台に、諏訪御料人の供養塔が建てられた。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ 岡谷市役所（商業観光課）ホームページ「由布姫のふるさと 小坂観音院（龍光山観音院）」]
{{DEFAULTSORT:かんのんいん}} [[category:長野県の寺]] [[Category:真言宗智山派]] [[category:岡谷市]] {{buddhism-stub}}

[[昭和]]38年（[[1963年]]）境内の高台に、諏訪御料人の供養塔が建てられた。

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = <ruby><rb>観音院</rb><rp>(</rp><rt>かんのんいん</rt><rp>)</rp></ruby> |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=岡谷市}} |所在地 = [[長野県]][[岡谷市]][[湊 (岡谷市)|湊]]4丁目15-22 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|2|6.7|N|138|4|7.1|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |山号 = [[龍光山]] |院号 = [[観音院]] |宗旨 = [[新義真言宗]] |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = 十一面観音 |創建年 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = 龍光山観音院 |別称 = 小坂観音院 |札所等 = |文化財 = }} '''観音院'''（かんのんいん）は、[[長野県]][[岡谷市]][[湊 (岡谷市)|湊四丁目]]にある[[真言宗智山派]]の寺院。山号は龍光山。本尊は[[十一面観音]]。 一般的には「'''小坂観音院'''」の名で知られる。
== 由緒 == かつては[[諏訪大社]]の[[社坊]]であった。中世に[[諏訪氏]]の[[祈願所]]となった。[[諏訪湖]]を見渡せる眺望の良い高台にある。
[[井上靖]]の著書「[[風林火山 (小説)|風林火山]]」では、[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]の娘[[諏訪御料人|由布姫]]（諏訪御料人）は、[[武田信玄]]の側室となって[[武田勝頼]]を生み、この観音院で暮らす設定になっている。井上靖の小説を原作とした[[平成]]19年（[[2007年]]）の[[日本放送協会|NHK]][[大河ドラマ]]「[[風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)|風林火山]]」でも観音院が登場する。
[[昭和]]38年（[[1963年]]）境内の高台に、諏訪御料人の供養塔が建てられた。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ 岡谷市役所（商業観光課）ホームページ「由布姫のふるさと 小坂観音院（龍光山観音院）」] == 関連項目 == * [[小坂城 (岡谷市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:かんのんいん}} [[Category:長野県の寺]] [[Category:真言宗智山派の寺院]] [[Category:諏訪信仰]] [[Category:神宮寺]] [[Category:諏訪氏]] [[Category:岡谷市の歴史]] [[Category:岡谷市の建築物]] {{buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{shinto-stub}}

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = <ruby><rb>観音院</rb><rp>(</rp><rt>かんのんいん</rt><rp>)</rp></ruby> |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=岡谷市}} |所在地 = [[長野県]][[岡谷市]][[湊 (岡谷市)|湊]]4丁目15-22 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|2|6.7|N|138|4|7.1|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |山号 = [[龍光山]] |院号 = [[観音院]] |宗旨 = [[新義真言宗]] |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = 十一面観音 |創建年 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = 龍光山観音院 |別称 = 小坂観音院 |札所等 = |文化財 = }} '''観音院'''（かんのんいん）は、[[長野県]][[岡谷市]][[湊 (岡谷市)|湊]]四丁目にある[[真言宗智山派]]の寺院。山号は龍光山。本尊は[[十一面観音]]。 一般的には「'''小坂観音院'''」の名で知られる。
== 由緒 == かつては[[諏訪大社]]の[[社坊]]であった。中世に[[諏訪氏]]の[[祈願所]]となった。[[諏訪湖]]を見渡せる眺望の良い高台にある。
[[井上靖]]の著書「[[風林火山 (小説)|風林火山]]」では、[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]の娘[[諏訪御料人|由布姫]]（諏訪御料人）は、[[武田信玄]]の側室となって[[武田勝頼]]を生み、この観音院で暮らす設定になっている。井上靖の小説を原作とした[[平成]]19年（[[2007年]]）の[[日本放送協会|NHK]][[大河ドラマ]]「[[風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)|風林火山]]」でも観音院が登場する。
[[昭和]]38年（[[1963年]]）境内の高台に、諏訪御料人の供養塔が建てられた。
== 参考文献 == *『探訪 信州の古寺 天台宗・真言宗』1996年 [[郷土出版社]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ 岡谷市役所（商業観光課）ホームページ「由布姫のふるさと 小坂観音院（龍光山観音院）」] == 関連項目 == * [[小坂城 (岡谷市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:かんのんいん}} [[Category:長野県の寺]] [[Category:真言宗智山派の寺院]] [[Category:諏訪信仰]] [[Category:神宮寺]] [[Category:諏訪氏]] [[Category:岡谷市の歴史]] [[Category:岡谷市の建築物]] {{buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{shinto-stub}}

== 由緒 == かつては[[諏訪大社]]の[[社坊]]であった。中世に[[諏訪氏]]の[[祈願所]]となった。[[諏訪湖]]を見渡せる眺望の良い高台にある。
[[井上靖]]の著書「[[風林火山 (小説)|風林火山]]」では、[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]の娘[[諏訪御料人|由布姫]]（諏訪御料人）は、[[武田信玄]]の側室となって[[武田勝頼]]を生み、この観音院で暮らす設定になっている。井上靖の小説を原作とした[[平成]]19年（[[2007年]]）の[[日本放送協会|NHK]][[大河ドラマ]]「[[風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)|風林火山]]」でも観音院が登場する。

{{RFD notice|'''対象リダイレクト:'''[[Wikipedia:リダイレクトの削除依頼/受付#RFD龍光山観音院|龍光山観音院（受付依頼）]]|※編集上のご注意！このテンプレートはBotによって自動貼付、および審議終了後およそ1週間で自動除去されます。審議途中でリダイレクト先を変更した場合や記事に置き換えた場合、またリダイレクトが削除された後でもテンプレートを取り除かないでください。前後に使用されているnoinclude文も同様です。手動で取り除いた場合、再度自動貼付されることがあります。}} {{日本の寺院 |名称 = {{ruby|観音院|かんのんいん}} |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=岡谷市}} |所在地 = [[長野県]][[岡谷市]][[湊 (岡谷市)|湊]]4丁目15-22 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|2|6.7|N|138|4|7.1|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |山号 = 龍光山 |院号 = 観音院 |宗旨 = [[新義真言宗]] |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = 十一面観音 |創建年 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = 龍光山観音院 |別称 = 小坂観音院 |札所等 = |文化財 = }}

revision.text:
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = {{ruby|観音院|かんのんいん}} |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=岡谷市}} |所在地 = [[長野県]][[岡谷市]][[湊 (岡谷市)|湊]]4丁目15-22 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|2|6.7|N|138|4|7.1|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |山号 = 龍光山 |院号 = 観音院 |宗旨 = [[新義真言宗]] |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = 十一面観音 |創建年 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = 龍光山観音院 |別称 = 小坂観音院 |札所等 = |文化財 = }}
'''観音院'''（かんのんいん）は、[[長野県]][[岡谷市]][[湊 (岡谷市)|湊]]四丁目にある[[真言宗智山派]]の寺院。山号は龍光山。本尊は[[十一面観音]]。一般的には「'''小坂観音院'''」の名で知られる。
== 由緒 == かつては[[諏訪大社]]の[[社坊]]であった。中世に[[諏訪氏]]の[[祈願所]]となった。[[諏訪湖]]を見渡せる眺望の良い高台にある。[[諏訪藩]]主[[諏訪忠恒]]を開基として中興された。本堂は正徳5年（1715年）[[諏訪忠虎]]によって再建された。
[[井上靖]]の著書「[[風林火山 (小説)|風林火山]]」では、[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]の娘[[諏訪御料人|由布姫]]（諏訪御料人）は、[[武田信玄]]の側室となって[[武田勝頼]]を生み、この観音院で暮らす設定になっている。井上靖の小説を原作とした[[平成]]19年（[[2007年]]）の[[日本放送協会|NHK]][[大河ドラマ]]「[[風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)|風林火山]]」でも観音院が登場する。
[[昭和]]38年（[[1963年]]）境内の高台に、諏訪御料人の供養塔が建てられた。
== 参考文献 == *『信州の文化シリーズ 寺と神社』1981年 [[信濃毎日新聞|信濃毎日新聞社]] *『探訪 信州の古寺 天台宗・真言宗』1996年 [[郷土出版社]]
== 関連項目 == * [[小坂城 (岡谷市)]] * [[湊村 (長野県)|湊村]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ 岡谷市役所（商業観光課）ホームページ「由布姫のふるさと 小坂観音院（龍光山観音院）」]
{{buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: 曖昧追加
revision.text:
{{otheruses|長野県岡谷市の寺院|その他の観音院|観音院 (曖昧さ回避)}} {{日本の寺院 |名称 = {{ruby|観音院|かんのんいん}} |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=岡谷市}} |所在地 = [[長野県]][[岡谷市]][[湊 (岡谷市)|湊]]4丁目15-22 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|2|6.7|N|138|4|7.1|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |山号 = 龍光山 |院号 = 観音院 |宗旨 = [[新義真言宗]] |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = 十一面観音 |創建年 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = 龍光山観音院 |別称 = 小坂観音院 |札所等 =[[伊那諏訪八十八霊場]]29番 |文化財 = }} '''観音院'''（かんのんいん）は、[[長野県]][[岡谷市]][[湊 (岡谷市)|湊]]四丁目にある[[真言宗智山派]]の寺院。山号は龍光山。本尊は[[十一面観音]]。一般的には「'''小坂観音院'''」の名で知られる。
== 由緒 == かつては[[諏訪大社]]の[[社坊]]であった。中世に[[諏訪氏]]の[[祈願所]]となった。[[諏訪湖]]を見渡せる眺望の良い高台にある。[[諏訪藩]]主[[諏訪忠恒]]を開基として中興された。本堂は正徳5年（1715年）[[諏訪忠虎]]によって再建された。
[[井上靖]]の著書「[[風林火山 (小説)|風林火山]]」では、[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]の娘[[諏訪御料人|由布姫]]（諏訪御料人）は、[[武田信玄]]の側室となって[[武田勝頼]]を生み、この観音院で暮らす設定になっている。井上靖の小説を原作とした[[平成]]19年（[[2007年]]）の[[日本放送協会|NHK]][[大河ドラマ]]「[[風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)|風林火山]]」でも観音院が登場する。
[[昭和]]38年（[[1963年]]）境内の高台に、諏訪御料人の供養塔が建てられた。
== 参考文献 == *『信州の文化シリーズ 寺と神社』1981年 [[信濃毎日新聞|信濃毎日新聞社]] *『探訪 信州の古寺 天台宗・真言宗』1996年 [[郷土出版社]]
== 関連項目 == * [[小坂城 (岡谷市)]] * [[湊村 (長野県)|湊村]] * [[岡谷市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット|岡谷市の名所・旧跡・観光スポット]] *[[岡谷市の歴史]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ 岡谷市役所（商業観光課）ホームページ「由布姫のふるさと 小坂観音院（龍光山観音院）」]

[[井上靖]]の著書「[[風林火山 (小説)|風林火山]]」では、[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]の娘[[諏訪御料人|由布姫]]（諏訪御料人）は、[[武田信玄]]の側室となって[[武田勝頼]]を生み、この観音院で暮らす設定になっている。井上靖の小説を原作とした[[平成]]19年（[[2007年]]）の[[日本放送協会|NHK]][[大河ドラマ]]「[[風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)|風林火山]]」でも観音院が登場する。
[[昭和]]38年（[[1963年]]）境内の高台に、諏訪御料人の供養塔が建てられた。

{{Infobox Buddhist Temple | name = Kannoni-in | image = {{Image requested|cat=岡谷市}} | location = Minato 4-chome 15-22, Okaya, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | latitude_deg = 36 | latitude_min = 2 | latitude_sec = 6.7 | longitude_deg = 138 | longitude_min = 4 | longitude_sec = 7.1 | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} | deity = {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}} | founded = | founder = | full_name = Ryūkōzan Kannoni-in | former_affiliation = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} (Shabō) | noteworthy = Historical ties to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}} | map = Nagano Prefecture | map_relief = yes | map_width = 256 }} '''Kannoni-in''' (観音院), also known as '''Kosaka Kannoni-in''', is a Buddhist temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} sect located in {{ill|Okaya|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is '''Ryūkōzan''' (龍光山), and its principal object of worship is the {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}}.
== History == Originally a {{ill|Shabō|ja|社坊}} (a temple associated with a Shinto shrine) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, Kannoni-in served as a {{ill|Prayer Temple|ja|祈願所}} for the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}} during the medieval period. The temple is situated on a hill overlooking {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, providing a scenic view of the surrounding area.
The temple was revived during the early Edo period under the patronage of {{ill|Suwa Tadatane|ja|諏訪忠恒}}, the lord of the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}}. The current main hall was rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by {{ill|Suwa Tadatora|ja|諏訪忠虎}}.
The temple is featured in {{ill|Yasushi Inoue|ja|井上靖}}'s historical novel *{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (novel)|ja|風林火山 (小説)}}*, where it serves as the residence of {{ill|Yufu-hime|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a concubine of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}} and the mother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}. The 2007 NHK {{ill|Taiga Drama|ja|大河ドラマ}} adaptation of *Fūrin Kazan* also portrays the temple.
In 1963 (Shōwa 38), a memorial tower dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin was erected on the temple grounds.
== Cultural Properties == * **Main Hall** - Rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by Suwa Tadatora. * **Memorial Tower** - Dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin in 1963.
== References == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines'' (1981). Shinano Mainichi Shimbun. * ''Exploring Shinshu's Ancient Temples: Tendai and Shingon Sects'' (1996). Kyodo Publishing.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kosaka Castle|ja|小坂城 (岡谷市)}} * {{ill|Minato Village|ja|湊村 (長野県)}} * {{ill|Okaya City Landmarks|ja|岡谷市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ Okaya City Tourism - Kosaka Kannoni-in]

The 2007 NHK {{ill|Taiga Drama|ja|大河ドラマ}} adaptation of *Fūrin Kazan* also portrays the temple.

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Kannoni-in | image = {{Image requested|cat=岡谷市}} | location = Minato 4-chome 15-22, Okaya, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | latitude_deg = 36 | latitude_min = 2 | latitude_sec = 6.7 | longitude_deg = 138 | longitude_min = 4 | longitude_sec = 7.1 | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} | deity = {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}} | founded = | founder = | full_name = Ryūkōzan Kannoni-in | former_affiliation = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} (Shabō) | noteworthy = Historical ties to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}} | map = Nagano Prefecture | map_relief = yes | map_width = 256 }} '''Kannoni-in''' (観音院), also known as '''Kosaka Kannoni-in''', is a Buddhist temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} sect located in {{ill|Okaya|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is '''Ryūkōzan''' (龍光山), and its principal object of worship is the {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}}.
== History == Originally a {{ill|Shabō|ja|社坊}} (a temple associated with a Shinto shrine) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, Kannoni-in served as a {{ill|Prayer Temple|ja|祈願所}} for the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}} during the medieval period. The temple is situated on a hill overlooking {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, providing a scenic view of the surrounding area.
The temple was revived during the early Edo period under the patronage of {{ill|Suwa Tadatane|ja|諏訪忠恒}}, the lord of the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}}. The current main hall was rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by {{ill|Suwa Tadatora|ja|諏訪忠虎}}.
The temple is featured in {{ill|Yasushi Inoue|ja|井上靖}}'s historical novel *{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (novel)|ja|風林火山 (小説)}}*, where it serves as the residence of {{ill|Yufu-hime|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a concubine of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}} and the mother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}. The 2007 NHK {{ill|Taiga Drama|ja|大河ドラマ}} adaptation of *Fūrin Kazan* also portrays the temple.
In 1963 (Shōwa 38), a memorial tower dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin was erected on the temple grounds.
== Cultural Properties == * **Main Hall** - Rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by Suwa Tadatora. * **Memorial Tower** - Dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin in 1963.
== References == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines'' (1981). Shinano Mainichi Shimbun. * ''Exploring Shinshu's Ancient Temples: Tendai and Shingon Sects'' (1996). Kyodo Publishing.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kosaka Castle|ja|小坂城 (岡谷市)}} * {{ill|Minato Village|ja|湊村 (長野県)}} * {{ill|Okaya City Landmarks|ja|岡谷市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ Okaya City Tourism - Kosaka Kannoni-in]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Kannoni-in | image = {{image requested|cat=岡谷市}} | location = Minato 4-chome 15-22, Okaya, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | latitude_deg = 36 | latitude_min = 2 | latitude_sec = 6.7 | longitude_deg = 138 | longitude_min = 4 | longitude_sec = 7.1 | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} | deity = {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}} | founded = | founder = | full_name = Ryūkōzan Kannoni-in | former_affiliation = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} (Shabō) | noteworthy = Historical ties to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}} | map = Nagano Prefecture | map_relief = yes | map_width = 256 }} '''Kannoni-in''' (観音院), also known as '''Kosaka Kannoni-in''', is a Buddhist temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} sect located in {{ill|Okaya|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is '''Ryūkōzan''' (龍光山), and its principal object of worship is the {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}}.
== History == Originally a {{ill|Shabō|ja|社坊}} (a temple associated with a Shinto shrine) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, Kannoni-in served as a {{ill|Prayer Temple|ja|祈願所}} for the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}} during the medieval period. The temple is situated on a hill overlooking {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, providing a scenic view of the surrounding area.
The temple was revived during the early Edo period under the patronage of {{ill|Suwa Tadatane|ja|諏訪忠恒}}, the lord of the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}}. The current main hall was rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by {{ill|Suwa Tadatora|ja|諏訪忠虎}}.
The temple is featured in {{ill|Yasushi Inoue|ja|井上靖}}'s historical novel *{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (novel)|ja|風林火山 (小説)}}*, where it serves as the residence of {{ill|Yufu-hime|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a concubine of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}} and the mother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}. The 2007 NHK {{ill|Taiga Drama|ja|大河ドラマ}} adaptation of *Fūrin Kazan* also portrays the temple.
In 1963 (Shōwa 38), a memorial tower dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin was erected on the temple grounds.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' - Rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by Suwa Tadatora. * '''Memorial Tower''' - Dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin in 1963.
== References == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines'' (1981). Shinano Mainichi Shimbun. * ''Exploring Shinshu's Ancient Temples: Tendai and Shingon Sects'' (1996). Kyodo Publishing.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kosaka Castle|ja|小坂城 (岡谷市)}} * {{ill|Minato Village|ja|湊村 (長野県)}} * {{ill|Okaya City Landmarks|ja|岡谷市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ Okaya City Tourism - Kosaka Kannoni-in]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Kannoni-in | image = {{image requested|cat=岡谷市}} | location = Minato 4-chome 15-22, Okaya, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | latitude_deg = 36 | latitude_min = 2 | latitude_sec = 6.7 | longitude_deg = 138 | longitude_min = 4 | longitude_sec = 7.1 | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} | deity = {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}} | founded = | founder = | full_name = Ryūkōzan Kannoni-in | former_affiliation = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} (Shabō) | noteworthy = Historical ties to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}} | map = Nagano Prefecture | map_relief = yes | map_width = 256 }} '''Kannoni-in''' (観音院), also known as '''Kosaka Kannoni-in''', is a Buddhist temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} sect located in {{ill|Okaya|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is '''Ryūkōzan''' (龍光山), and its principal object of worship is the {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}}.
== History == Originally a {{ill|Shabō|ja|社坊}} (a temple associated with a Shinto shrine) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, Kannoni-in served as a {{ill|Prayer Temple|ja|祈願所}} for the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}} during the medieval period. The temple is situated on a hill overlooking {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, providing a scenic view of the surrounding area.
The temple was revived during the early Edo period under the patronage of {{ill|Suwa Tadatane|ja|諏訪忠恒}}, the lord of the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}}. The current main hall was rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by {{ill|Suwa Tadatora|ja|諏訪忠虎}}.
The temple is featured in {{ill|Yasushi Inoue|ja|井上靖}}'s historical novel *{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (novel)|ja|風林火山 (小説)}}*, where it serves as the residence of {{ill|Yufu-hime|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a concubine of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}} and the mother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}. The 2007 NHK {{ill|Taiga Drama|ja|大河ドラマ}} adaptation of *Fūrin Kazan* also portrays the temple.
In 1963 (Shōwa 38), a memorial tower dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin was erected on the temple grounds.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' - Rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by Suwa Tadatora. * '''Memorial Tower''' - Dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin in 1963.
== References == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines'' (1981). Shinano Mainichi Shimbun. * ''Exploring Shinshu's Ancient Temples: Tendai and Shingon Sects'' (1996). Kyodo Publishing.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kosaka Castle|ja|小坂城 (岡谷市)}} * {{ill|Minato Village|ja|湊村 (長野県)}} * {{ill|Okaya City Landmarks|ja|岡谷市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ Okaya City Tourism - Kosaka Kannoni-in]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Kannoni-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=岡谷市}} | location = Minato 4-chome 15-22, Okaya, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | latitude_deg = 36 | latitude_min = 2 | latitude_sec = 6.7 | longitude_deg = 138 | longitude_min = 4 | longitude_sec = 7.1 | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} | deity = {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}} | founded = | founder = | full_name = Ryūkōzan Kannoni-in | former_affiliation = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} (Shabō) | noteworthy = Historical ties to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}} | map = Nagano Prefecture | map_relief = yes | map_width = 256 }} '''Kannoni-in''' (観音院), also known as '''Kosaka Kannoni-in''', is a Buddhist temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} sect located in {{ill|Okaya|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is '''Ryūkōzan''' (龍光山), and its principal object of worship is the {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}}.
== History == Originally a {{ill|Shabō|ja|社坊}} (a temple associated with a Shinto shrine) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, Kannoni-in served as a {{ill|Prayer Temple|ja|祈願所}} for the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}} during the medieval period. The temple is situated on a hill overlooking {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, providing a scenic view of the surrounding area.
The temple was revived during the early Edo period under the patronage of {{ill|Suwa Tadatane|ja|諏訪忠恒}}, the lord of the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}}. The current main hall was rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by {{ill|Suwa Tadatora|ja|諏訪忠虎}}.
The temple is featured in {{ill|Yasushi Inoue|ja|井上靖}}'s historical novel *{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (novel)|ja|風林火山 (小説)}}*, where it serves as the residence of {{ill|Yufu-hime|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a concubine of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}} and the mother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}. The 2007 NHK {{ill|Taiga Drama|ja|大河ドラマ}} adaptation of *Fūrin Kazan* also portrays the temple.
In 1963 (Shōwa 38), a memorial tower dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin was erected on the temple grounds.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' - Rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by Suwa Tadatora. * '''Memorial Tower''' - Dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin in 1963.
== References == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines'' (1981). Shinano Mainichi Shimbun. * ''Exploring Shinshu's Ancient Temples: Tendai and Shingon Sects'' (1996). Kyodo Publishing.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kosaka Castle|ja|小坂城 (岡谷市)}} * {{ill|Minato Village|ja|湊村 (長野県)}} * {{ill|Okaya City Landmarks|ja|岡谷市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ Okaya City Tourism - Kosaka Kannoni-in]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Kannoni-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=岡谷市}} | location = Minato 4-chome 15-22, Okaya, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | latitude_deg = 36 | latitude_min = 2 | latitude_sec = 6.7 | longitude_deg = 138 | longitude_min = 4 | longitude_sec = 7.1 | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} | deity = {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}} | founded = | founder = | full_name = Ryūkōzan Kannoni-in | former_affiliation = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} (Shabō) | noteworthy = Historical ties to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}} | map = Nagano Prefecture | map_relief = yes | map_width = 256 }} '''Kannoni-in''' (観音院), also known as '''Kosaka Kannoni-in''', is a Buddhist temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} sect located in {{ill|Okaya|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is '''Ryūkōzan''' (龍光山), and its principal object of worship is the {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}}.
== History == Originally a {{ill|Shabō|ja|社坊}} (a temple associated with a Shinto shrine) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, Kannoni-in served as a {{ill|Prayer Temple|ja|祈願所}} for the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}} during the medieval period. The temple is situated on a hill overlooking {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, providing a scenic view of the surrounding area.
The temple was revived during the early Edo period under the patronage of {{ill|Suwa Tadatane|ja|諏訪忠恒}}, the lord of the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}}. The current main hall was rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by {{ill|Suwa Tadatora|ja|諏訪忠虎}}.
The temple is featured in {{ill|Yasushi Inoue|ja|井上靖}}'s historical novel *{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (novel)|ja|風林火山 (小説)}}*, where it serves as the residence of {{ill|Yufu-hime|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a concubine of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}} and the mother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}. The 2007 NHK {{ill|Taiga Drama|ja|大河ドラマ}} adaptation of *Fūrin Kazan* also portrays the temple.
In 1963 (Shōwa 38), a memorial tower dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin was erected on the temple grounds.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' - Rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by Suwa Tadatora. * '''Memorial Tower''' - Dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin in 1963.
== References == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines'' (1981). Shinano Mainichi Shimbun. * ''Exploring Shinshu's Ancient Temples: Tendai and Shingon Sects'' (1996). Kyodo Publishing.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kosaka Castle|ja|小坂城 (岡谷市)}} * {{ill|Minato Village|ja|湊村 (長野県)}} * {{ill|Okaya City Landmarks|ja|岡谷市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ Okaya City Tourism - Kosaka Kannoni-in]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Kannoni-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=岡谷市}} | location = Minato 4-chome 15-22, Okaya, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | latitude_deg = 36 | latitude_min = 2 | latitude_sec = 6.7 | longitude_deg = 138 | longitude_min = 4 | longitude_sec = 7.1 | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} | deity = {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}} | founded = | founder = | full_name = Ryūkōzan Kannoni-in | former_affiliation = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} (Shabō) | noteworthy = Historical ties to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}} | map = Nagano Prefecture | map_relief = yes | map_width = 256 }} '''Kannoni-in''' (観音院), also known as '''Kosaka Kannoni-in''', is a Buddhist temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} sect located in {{ill|Okaya|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is '''Ryūkōzan''' (龍光山), and its principal object of worship is the {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}}.
== History == Originally a {{ill|Shabō|ja|社坊}} (a temple associated with a Shinto shrine) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, Kannoni-in served as a {{ill|Prayer Temple|ja|祈願所}} for the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}} during the medieval period. The temple is situated on a hill overlooking {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, providing a scenic view of the surrounding area.
The temple was revived during the early Edo period under the patronage of {{ill|Suwa Tadatane|ja|諏訪忠恒}}, the lord of the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}}. The current main hall was rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by {{ill|Suwa Tadatora|ja|諏訪忠虎}}.
The temple is featured in {{ill|Yasushi Inoue|ja|井上靖}}'s historical novel *{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (novel)|ja|風林火山 (小説)}}*, where it serves as the residence of {{ill|Yufu-hime|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a concubine of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}} and the mother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}. The 2007 NHK {{ill|Taiga Drama|ja|大河ドラマ}} adaptation of *Fūrin Kazan* also portrays the temple.
In 1963 (Shōwa 38), a memorial tower dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin was erected on the temple grounds.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' - Rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by Suwa Tadatora. * '''Memorial Tower''' - Dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin in 1963.
== References == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines'' (1981). Shinano Mainichi Shimbun. * ''Exploring Shinshu's Ancient Temples: Tendai and Shingon Sects'' (1996). Kyodo Publishing.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kosaka Castle|ja|小坂城 (岡谷市)}} * {{ill|Minato Village|ja|湊村 (長野県)}} * {{ill|Okaya City Landmarks|ja|岡谷市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ Okaya City Tourism - Kosaka Kannoni-in]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kannoni-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=岡谷市}} | location = Minato 4-chome 15-22, Okaya, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | latitude_deg = 36 | latitude_min = 2 | latitude_sec = 6.7 | longitude_deg = 138 | longitude_min = 4 | longitude_sec = 7.1 | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} | deity = {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}} | founded = | founder = | full_name = Ryūkōzan Kannoni-in | former_affiliation = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} (Shabō) | noteworthy = Historical ties to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}} | map = Nagano Prefecture | map_relief = yes | map_width = 256 }} '''Kannoni-in''' (観音院), also known as '''Kosaka Kannoni-in''', is a Buddhist temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} sect located in {{ill|Okaya|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is '''Ryūkōzan''' (龍光山), and its principal object of worship is the {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}}.
== History == Originally a {{ill|Shabō|ja|社坊}} (a temple associated with a Shinto shrine) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, Kannoni-in served as a {{ill|Prayer Temple|ja|祈願所}} for the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}} during the medieval period. The temple is situated on a hill overlooking {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, providing a scenic view of the surrounding area.
The temple was revived during the early Edo period under the patronage of {{ill|Suwa Tadatane|ja|諏訪忠恒}}, the lord of the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}}. The current main hall was rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by {{ill|Suwa Tadatora|ja|諏訪忠虎}}.
The temple is featured in {{ill|Yasushi Inoue|ja|井上靖}}'s historical novel *{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (novel)|ja|風林火山 (小説)}}*, where it serves as the residence of {{ill|Yufu-hime|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a concubine of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}} and the mother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}. The 2007 NHK {{ill|Taiga Drama|ja|大河ドラマ}} adaptation of *Fūrin Kazan* also portrays the temple.
In 1963 (Shōwa 38), a memorial tower dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin was erected on the temple grounds.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' - Rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by Suwa Tadatora. * '''Memorial Tower''' - Dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin in 1963.
== References == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines'' (1981). Shinano Mainichi Shimbun. * ''Exploring Shinshu's Ancient Temples: Tendai and Shingon Sects'' (1996). Kyodo Publishing.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kosaka Castle|ja|小坂城 (岡谷市)}} * {{ill|Minato Village|ja|湊村 (長野県)}} * {{ill|Okaya City Landmarks|ja|岡谷市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ Okaya City Tourism - Kosaka Kannoni-in]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kannoni-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=岡谷市}} | location = Minato 4-chome 15-22, Okaya, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | latitude_deg = 36 | latitude_min = 2 | latitude_sec = 6.7 | longitude_deg = 138 | longitude_min = 4 | longitude_sec = 7.1 | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} | deity = {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}} | founded = | founder = | full_name = Ryūkōzan Kannoni-in | former_affiliation = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} (Shabō) | noteworthy = Historical ties to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}} | map = Nagano Prefecture | map_relief = yes | map_width = 256 }} '''Kannoni-in''' (観音院), also known as '''Kosaka Kannoni-in''', is a Buddhist temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} sect located in {{ill|Okaya|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is '''Ryūkōzan''' (龍光山), and its principal object of worship is the {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}}.
== History == Originally a {{ill|Shabō|ja|社坊}} (a temple associated with a Shinto shrine) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, Kannoni-in served as a {{ill|Prayer Temple|ja|祈願所}} for the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}} during the medieval period. The temple is situated on a hill overlooking {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, providing a scenic view of the surrounding area.
The temple was revived during the early Edo period under the patronage of {{ill|Suwa Tadatane|ja|諏訪忠恒}}, the lord of the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}}. The current main hall was rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by {{ill|Suwa Tadatora|ja|諏訪忠虎}}.
The temple is featured in {{ill|Yasushi Inoue|ja|井上靖}}'s historical novel *{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (novel)|ja|風林火山 (小説)}}*, where it serves as the residence of {{ill|Yufu-hime|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a concubine of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}} and the mother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}. The 2007 NHK {{ill|Taiga Drama|ja|大河ドラマ}} adaptation of *Fūrin Kazan* also portrays the temple.
In 1963 (Shōwa 38), a memorial tower dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin was erected on the temple grounds.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' - Rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by Suwa Tadatora. * '''Memorial Tower''' - Dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin in 1963.
== References == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines'' (1981). Shinano Mainichi Shimbun. * ''Exploring Shinshu's Ancient Temples: Tendai and Shingon Sects'' (1996). Kyodo Publishing.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kosaka Castle|ja|小坂城 (岡谷市)}} * {{ill|Minato Village|ja|湊村 (長野県)}} * {{ill|Okaya City Landmarks|ja|岡谷市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ Okaya City Tourism - Kosaka Kannoni-in]

The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is '''Ryūkōzan''' (龍光山), and its principal object of worship is the {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}}.
== History == Originally a {{ill|Shabō|ja|社坊}} (a temple associated with a Shinto shrine) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, Kannoni-in served as a {{ill|Prayer Temple|ja|祈願所}} for the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}} during the medieval period. The temple is situated on a hill overlooking {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, providing a scenic view of the surrounding area.
The temple was revived during the early Edo period under the patronage of {{ill|Suwa Tadatane|ja|諏訪忠恒}}, the lord of the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}}. The current main hall was rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by {{ill|Suwa Tadatora|ja|諏訪忠虎}}.
The temple is featured in {{ill|Yasushi Inoue|ja|井上靖}}'s historical novel *{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (novel)|ja|風林火山 (小説)}}*, where it serves as the residence of {{ill|Yufu-hime|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a concubine of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}} and the mother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}. The 2007 NHK {{ill|Taiga Drama|ja|大河ドラマ}} adaptation of *Fūrin Kazan* also portrays the temple.
In 1963 (Shōwa 38), a memorial tower dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin was erected on the temple grounds.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' - Rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by Suwa Tadatora. * '''Memorial Tower''' - Dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin in 1963.
== References == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines'' (1981). Shinano Mainichi Shimbun. * ''Exploring Shinshu's Ancient Temples: Tendai and Shingon Sects'' (1996). Kyodo Publishing.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kosaka Castle|ja|小坂城 (岡谷市)}} * {{ill|Minato Village|ja|湊村 (長野県)}} * {{ill|Okaya City Landmarks|ja|岡谷市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ Okaya City Tourism - Kosaka Kannoni-in]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kannoni-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=岡谷市}} | location = Minato 4-chome 15-22, Okaya, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | latitude_deg = 36 | latitude_min = 2 | latitude_sec = 6.7 | longitude_deg = 138 | longitude_min = 4 | longitude_sec = 7.1 | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} | deity = {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}} | founded = | founder = | full_name = Ryūkōzan Kannoni-in | former_affiliation = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} (Shabō) | noteworthy = Historical ties to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}} | map = Nagano Prefecture | map_relief = yes | map_width = 256 }} '''Kannoni-in''' (観音院), also known as '''Kosaka Kannoni-in''', is a Buddhist temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} sect located in {{ill|Okaya|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is '''Ryūkōzan''' (龍光山), and its principal object of worship is the {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}}.
== History == Originally a {{ill|Shabō|ja|社坊}} (a temple associated with a Shinto shrine) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, Kannoni-in served as a {{ill|Prayer Temple|ja|祈願所}} for the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}} during the medieval period. The temple is situated on a hill overlooking {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, providing a scenic view of the surrounding area.
The temple was revived during the early Edo period under the patronage of {{ill|Suwa Tadatane|ja|諏訪忠恒}}, the lord of the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}}. The current main hall was rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by {{ill|Suwa Tadatora|ja|諏訪忠虎}}.
The temple is featured in {{ill|Yasushi Inoue|ja|井上靖}}'s historical novel *{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (novel)|ja|風林火山 (小説)}}*, where it serves as the residence of {{ill|Yufu-hime|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a concubine of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}} and the mother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}. The 2007 NHK {{ill|Taiga Drama|ja|大河ドラマ}} adaptation of *Fūrin Kazan* also portrays the temple.
In 1963 (Shōwa 38), a memorial tower dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin was erected on the temple grounds.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' - Rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by Suwa Tadatora. * '''Memorial Tower''' - Dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin in 1963.
== References == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines'' (1981). Shinano Mainichi Shimbun. * ''Exploring Shinshu's Ancient Temples: Tendai and Shingon Sects'' (1996). Kyodo Publishing.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kosaka Castle|ja|小坂城 (岡谷市)}} * {{ill|Minato Village|ja|湊村 (長野県)}} * {{ill|Okaya City Landmarks|ja|岡谷市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ Okaya City Tourism - Kosaka Kannoni-in]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kannoni-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=岡谷市}} | location = Minato 4-chome 15-22, Okaya, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | latitude_deg = 36 | latitude_min = 2 | latitude_sec = 6.7 | longitude_deg = 138 | longitude_min = 4 | longitude_sec = 7.1 | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} | deity = {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}} | founded = | founder = | full_name = Ryūkōzan Kannoni-in | former_affiliation = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} (Shabō) | noteworthy = Historical ties to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}} | map = Nagano Prefecture | map_relief = yes | map_width = 256 }} '''Kannoni-in''' (観音院), also known as '''Kosaka Kannoni-in''', is a Buddhist temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} sect located in {{ill|Okaya|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is '''Ryūkōzan''' (龍光山), and its principal object of worship is the {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}}.
== History == Originally a {{ill|Shabō|ja|社坊}} (a temple associated with a Shinto shrine) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, Kannoni-in served as a {{ill|Prayer Temple|ja|祈願所}} for the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}} during the medieval period. The temple is situated on a hill overlooking {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, providing a scenic view of the surrounding area.
The temple was revived during the early Edo period under the patronage of {{ill|Suwa Tadatane|ja|諏訪忠恒}}, the lord of the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}}. The current main hall was rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by {{ill|Suwa Tadatora|ja|諏訪忠虎}}.
The temple is featured in {{ill|Yasushi Inoue|ja|井上靖}}'s historical novel *{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (novel)|ja|風林火山 (小説)}}*, where it serves as the residence of {{ill|Yufu-hime|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a concubine of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}} and the mother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}. The 2007 NHK {{ill|Taiga Drama|ja|大河ドラマ}} adaptation of *Fūrin Kazan* also portrays the temple.
In 1963 (Shōwa 38), a memorial tower dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin was erected on the temple grounds.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' - Rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by Suwa Tadatora. * '''Memorial Tower''' - Dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin in 1963.
== References == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines'' (1981). Shinano Mainichi Shimbun. * ''Exploring Shinshu's Ancient Temples: Tendai and Shingon Sects'' (1996). Kyodo Publishing.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kosaka Castle|ja|小坂城 (岡谷市)}} * {{ill|Minato Village|ja|湊村 (長野県)}} * {{ill|Okaya City Landmarks|ja|岡谷市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ Okaya City Tourism - Kosaka Kannoni-in]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kannoni-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=岡谷市}} | location = Minato 4-chome 15-22, Okaya, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | latitude_deg = 36 | latitude_min = 2 | latitude_sec = 6.7 | longitude_deg = 138 | longitude_min = 4 | longitude_sec = 7.1 | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} | deity = {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}} | founded = | founder = | full_name = Ryūkōzan Kannoni-in | former_affiliation = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} (Shabō) | noteworthy = Historical ties to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}} | map = Nagano Prefecture | map_relief = yes | map_width = 256 }} '''Kannoni-in''' (観音院), also known as '''Kosaka Kannoni-in''', is a Buddhist temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} sect located in {{ill|Okaya|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is '''Ryūkōzan''' (龍光山), and its principal object of worship is the {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}}.
== History == Originally a {{ill|Shabō|ja|社坊}} (a temple associated with a Shinto shrine) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}, Kannoni-in served as a {{ill|Prayer Temple|ja|祈願所}} for the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan}} during the medieval period. The temple is situated on a hill overlooking {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, providing a scenic view of the surrounding area.
The temple was revived during the early Edo period under the patronage of {{ill|Suwa Tadatane|ja|諏訪忠恒}}, the lord of the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}}. The current main hall was rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by {{ill|Suwa Tadatora|ja|諏訪忠虎}}.
The temple is featured in {{ill|Yasushi Inoue|ja|井上靖}}'s historical novel *{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (novel)|ja|風林火山 (小説)}}*, where it serves as the residence of {{ill|Yufu-hime|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a concubine of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}} and the mother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}. The 2007 NHK {{ill|Taiga Drama|ja|大河ドラマ}} adaptation of *Fūrin Kazan* also portrays the temple.
In 1963 (Shōwa 38), a memorial tower dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin was erected on the temple grounds.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' - Rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by Suwa Tadatora. * '''Memorial Tower''' - Dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin in 1963.
== References == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines'' (1981). Shinano Mainichi Shimbun. * ''Exploring Shinshu's Ancient Temples: Tendai and Shingon Sects'' (1996). Kyodo Publishing.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kosaka Castle|ja|小坂城 (岡谷市)}} * {{ill|Minato Village|ja|湊村 (長野県)}} * {{ill|Okaya City Landmarks|ja|岡谷市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ Okaya City Tourism - Kosaka Kannoni-in]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kannoni-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=岡谷市}} | location = Minato 4-chome 15-22, Okaya, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | latitude_deg = 36 | latitude_min = 2 | latitude_sec = 6.7 | longitude_deg = 138 | longitude_min = 4 | longitude_sec = 7.1 | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} | deity = {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}} | founded = | founder = | full_name = Ryūkōzan Kannoni-in | former_affiliation = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} (Shabō) | noteworthy = Historical ties to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}} | map = Nagano Prefecture | map_relief = yes | map_width = 256 }} '''Kannoni-in''' (観音院), also known as '''Kosaka Kannoni-in''', is a Buddhist temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派}} sect located in {{ill|Okaya|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is '''Ryūkōzan''' (龍光山), and its principal object of worship is the {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}}.
== History == Originally a {{ill|Shabō|ja|社坊}} (a temple associated with a Shinto shrine) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}, Kannoni-in served as a {{ill|Prayer Temple|ja|祈願所}} for the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan}} during the medieval period. The temple is situated on a hill overlooking {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, providing a scenic view of the surrounding area.
The temple was revived during the early Edo period under the patronage of {{ill|Suwa Tadatane|ja|諏訪忠恒}}, the lord of the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}}. The current main hall was rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by {{ill|Suwa Tadatora|ja|諏訪忠虎}}.
The temple is featured in {{ill|Yasushi Inoue|ja|井上靖}}'s historical novel *{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (novel)|ja|風林火山 (小説)}}*, where it serves as the residence of {{ill|Yufu-hime|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a concubine of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}} and the mother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}. The 2007 NHK {{ill|Taiga Drama|ja|大河ドラマ}} adaptation of *Fūrin Kazan* also portrays the temple.
In 1963 (Shōwa 38), a memorial tower dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin was erected on the temple grounds.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' - Rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by Suwa Tadatora. * '''Memorial Tower''' - Dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin in 1963.
== References == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines'' (1981). Shinano Mainichi Shimbun. * ''Exploring Shinshu's Ancient Temples: Tendai and Shingon Sects'' (1996). Kyodo Publishing.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kosaka Castle|ja|小坂城 (岡谷市)}} * {{ill|Minato Village|ja|湊村 (長野県)}} * {{ill|Okaya City Landmarks|ja|岡谷市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ Okaya City Tourism - Kosaka Kannoni-in]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Kannoni-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=岡谷市}} | location = Minato 4-chome 15-22, Okaya, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | latitude_deg = 36 | latitude_min = 2 | latitude_sec = 6.7 | longitude_deg = 138 | longitude_min = 4 | longitude_sec = 7.1 | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} | deity = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}} | founded = | founder = | full_name = Ryūkōzan Kannoni-in | former_affiliation = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} (Shabō) | noteworthy = Historical ties to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}} | map = Nagano Prefecture | map_relief = yes | map_width = 256 }} '''Kannoni-in''' (観音院), also known as '''Kosaka Kannoni-in''', is a Buddhist temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} sect located in {{ill|Okaya|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is '''Ryūkōzan''' (龍光山), and its principal object of worship is the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}}.
== History == Originally a {{ill|Shabō|ja|社坊}} (a temple associated with a Shinto shrine) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}, Kannoni-in served as a {{ill|Prayer Temple|ja|祈願所}} for the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan}} during the medieval period. The temple is situated on a hill overlooking {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, providing a scenic view of the surrounding area.
The temple was revived during the early Edo period under the patronage of {{ill|Suwa Tadatane|ja|諏訪忠恒}}, the lord of the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}}. The current main hall was rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by {{ill|Suwa Tadatora|ja|諏訪忠虎}}.
The temple is featured in {{ill|Yasushi Inoue|ja|井上靖}}'s historical novel *{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (novel)|ja|風林火山 (小説)}}*, where it serves as the residence of {{ill|Yufu-hime|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a concubine of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen}} and the mother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}. The 2007 NHK {{ill|Taiga Drama|ja|大河ドラマ}} adaptation of *Fūrin Kazan* also portrays the temple.
In 1963 (Shōwa 38), a memorial tower dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin was erected on the temple grounds.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' - Rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by Suwa Tadatora. * '''Memorial Tower''' - Dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin in 1963.
== References == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines'' (1981). Shinano Mainichi Shimbun. * ''Exploring Shinshu's Ancient Temples: Tendai and Shingon Sects'' (1996). Kyodo Publishing.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kosaka Castle|ja|小坂城 (岡谷市)}} * {{ill|Minato Village|ja|湊村 (長野県)}} * {{ill|Okaya City Landmarks|ja|岡谷市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ Okaya City Tourism - Kosaka Kannoni-in]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kannoni-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=岡谷市}} | location = Minato 4-chome 15-22, Okaya, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | latitude_deg = 36 | latitude_min = 2 | latitude_sec = 6.7 | longitude_deg = 138 | longitude_min = 4 | longitude_sec = 7.1 | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} | deity = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}} | founded = | founder = | full_name = Ryūkōzan Kannoni-in | former_affiliation = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} (Shabō) | noteworthy = Historical ties to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}} | map = Nagano Prefecture | map_relief = yes | map_width = 256 }} '''Kannoni-in''' (観音院), also known as '''Kosaka Kannoni-in''', is a Buddhist temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} sect located in {{ill|Okaya|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is '''Ryūkōzan''' (龍光山), and its principal object of worship is the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}}.
== History == Originally a {{ill|Shabō|ja|社坊}} (a temple associated with a Shinto shrine) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}, Kannoni-in served as a {{ill|Prayer Temple|ja|祈願所}} for the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan}} during the medieval period. The temple is situated on a hill overlooking {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, providing a scenic view of the surrounding area.
The temple was revived during the early Edo period under the patronage of {{ill|Suwa Tadatane|ja|諏訪忠恒}}, the lord of the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}}. The current main hall was rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by {{ill|Suwa Tadatora|ja|諏訪忠虎}}.
The temple is featured in {{ill|Yasushi Inoue|ja|井上靖}}'s historical novel *{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (novel)|ja|風林火山 (小説)}}*, where it serves as the residence of {{ill|Yufu-hime|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a concubine of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen}} and the mother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}. The 2007 NHK {{ill|Taiga Drama|ja|大河ドラマ}} adaptation of *Fūrin Kazan* also portrays the temple.
In 1963 (Shōwa 38), a memorial tower dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin was erected on the temple grounds.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' - Rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by Suwa Tadatora. * '''Memorial Tower''' - Dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin in 1963.
== References == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines'' (1981). Shinano Mainichi Shimbun. * ''Exploring Shinshu's Ancient Temples: Tendai and Shingon Sects'' (1996). Kyodo Publishing.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kosaka Castle|ja|小坂城 (岡谷市)}} * {{ill|Minato Village|ja|湊村 (長野県)}} * {{ill|Okaya City Landmarks|ja|岡谷市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ Okaya City Tourism - Kosaka Kannoni-in]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kannoni-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=岡谷市}} | location = Minato 4-chome 15-22, Okaya, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | latitude_deg = 36 | latitude_min = 2 | latitude_sec = 6.7 | longitude_deg = 138 | longitude_min = 4 | longitude_sec = 7.1 | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} | deity = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}} | founded = | founder = | full_name = Ryūkōzan Kannoni-in | former_affiliation = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} (Shabō) | noteworthy = Historical ties to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}} | map = Nagano Prefecture | map_relief = yes | map_width = 256 }} '''Kannoni-in''' (観音院), also known as '''Kosaka Kannoni-in''', is a Buddhist temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} sect located in {{ill|Okaya|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is '''Ryūkōzan''' (龍光山), and its principal object of worship is the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}}.
== History == Originally a {{ill|Shabō|ja|社坊}} (a temple associated with a Shinto shrine) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}, Kannoni-in served as a {{ill|Prayer Temple|ja|祈願所|1=Kigan-sho}} for the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan}} during the medieval period. The temple is situated on a hill overlooking {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, providing a scenic view of the surrounding area.
The temple was revived during the early Edo period under the patronage of {{ill|Suwa Tadatane|ja|諏訪忠恒}}, the lord of the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}}. The current main hall was rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by {{ill|Suwa Tadatora|ja|諏訪忠虎}}.
The temple is featured in {{ill|Yasushi Inoue|ja|井上靖}}'s historical novel *{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (novel)|ja|風林火山 (小説)}}*, where it serves as the residence of {{ill|Yufu-hime|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a concubine of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen}} and the mother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}. The 2007 NHK {{ill|Taiga Drama|ja|大河ドラマ}} adaptation of *Fūrin Kazan* also portrays the temple.
In 1963 (Shōwa 38), a memorial tower dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin was erected on the temple grounds.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' - Rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by Suwa Tadatora. * '''Memorial Tower''' - Dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin in 1963.
== References == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines'' (1981). Shinano Mainichi Shimbun. * ''Exploring Shinshu's Ancient Temples: Tendai and Shingon Sects'' (1996). Kyodo Publishing.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kosaka Castle|ja|小坂城 (岡谷市)}} * {{ill|Minato Village|ja|湊村 (長野県)}} * {{ill|Okaya City Landmarks|ja|岡谷市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ Okaya City Tourism - Kosaka Kannoni-in]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Kannoni-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=岡谷市}} | location = Minato 4-chome 15-22, Okaya, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | latitude_deg = 36 | latitude_min = 2 | latitude_sec = 6.7 | longitude_deg = 138 | longitude_min = 4 | longitude_sec = 7.1 | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} | deity = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}} | founded = | founder = | full_name = Ryūkōzan Kannoni-in | former_affiliation = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} (Shabō) | noteworthy = Historical ties to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}} | map = Nagano Prefecture | map_relief = yes | map_width = 256 }} '''Kannoni-in''' (観音院), also known as '''Kosaka Kannoni-in''', is a Buddhist temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} sect located in {{ill|Okaya|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is '''Ryūkōzan''' (龍光山), and its principal object of worship is the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}}.
== History == Originally a {{ill|Shabō|ja|社坊}} (a temple associated with a Shinto shrine) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}, Kannoni-in served as a {{ill|Prayer Temple|ja|祈願所|1=Kigan-sho}} for the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan}} during the medieval period. The temple is situated on a hill overlooking {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, providing a scenic view of the surrounding area.
The temple was revived during the early Edo period under the patronage of {{ill|Suwa Tadatane|ja|諏訪忠恒}}, the lord of the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}}. The current main hall was rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by {{ill|Suwa Tadatora|ja|諏訪忠虎}}.
The temple is featured in {{ill|Yasushi Inoue|ja|井上靖}}'s historical novel *{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (novel)|ja|風林火山 (小説)}}*, where it serves as the residence of {{ill|Yufu-hime|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a concubine of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen}} and the mother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}. The 2007 NHK {{ill|Taiga Drama|ja|大河ドラマ}} adaptation of *Fūrin Kazan* also portrays the temple.
In 1963 (Shōwa 38), a memorial tower dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin was erected on the temple grounds.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' - Rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by Suwa Tadatora. * '''Memorial Tower''' - Dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin in 1963.
== References == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines'' (1981). Shinano Mainichi Shimbun. * ''Exploring Shinshu's Ancient Temples: Tendai and Shingon Sects'' (1996). Kyodo Publishing. == See Also == * {{ill|Kosaka Castle|ja|小坂城 (岡谷市)}} * {{ill|Minato Village|ja|湊村 (長野県)}} * {{ill|Okaya City Landmarks|ja|岡谷市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ Okaya City Tourism - Kosaka Kannoni-in]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Kannoni-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=岡谷市}} | location = Minato 4-chome 15-22, Okaya, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | latitude_deg = 36 | latitude_min = 2 | latitude_sec = 6.7 | longitude_deg = 138 | longitude_min = 4 | longitude_sec = 7.1 | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} | deity = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}} | founded = | founder = | full_name = Ryūkōzan Kannoni-in | former_affiliation = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} (Shabō) | noteworthy = Historical ties to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}} | map = Nagano Prefecture | map_relief = yes | map_width = 256 }} '''Kannoni-in''' (観音院), also known as '''Kosaka Kannoni-in''', is a Buddhist temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} sect located in {{ill|Okaya|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is '''Ryūkōzan''' (龍光山), and its principal object of worship is the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音}}.
== History == Originally a {{ill|Shabō|ja|社坊}} (a temple associated with a Shinto shrine) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}, Kannoni-in served as a {{ill|Prayer Temple|ja|祈願所|1=Kigan-sho}} for the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan}} during the medieval period. The temple is situated on a hill overlooking {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, providing a scenic view of the surrounding area.
The temple was revived during the early Edo period under the patronage of {{ill|Suwa Tadatane|ja|諏訪忠恒}}, the lord of the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}}. The current main hall was rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by {{ill|Suwa Tadatora|ja|諏訪忠虎}}.
The temple is featured in {{ill|Yasushi Inoue|ja|井上靖}}'s historical novel *{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (novel)|ja|風林火山 (小説)}}*, where it serves as the residence of {{ill|Yufu-hime|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a concubine of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen}} and the mother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}. The 2007 NHK {{ill|Taiga Drama|ja|大河ドラマ}} adaptation of *Fūrin Kazan* also portrays the temple.
In 1963 (Shōwa 38), a memorial tower dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin was erected on the temple grounds.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' - Rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by Suwa Tadatora. * '''Memorial Tower''' - Dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin in 1963.
== References == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines'' (1981). Shinano Mainichi Shimbun. * ''Exploring Shinshu's Ancient Temples: Tendai and Shingon Sects'' (1996). Kyodo Publishing. == See Also == * {{ill|Kosaka Castle|ja|小坂城 (岡谷市)}} * {{ill|Minato Village|ja|湊村 (長野県)}} * {{ill|Okaya City Landmarks|ja|岡谷市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ Okaya City Tourism - Kosaka Kannoni-in]

The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is '''Ryūkōzan''' (龍光山), and its principal object of worship is the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== History == Originally a {{ill|Shabō|ja|社坊|12=draft|12=draft}} (a temple associated with a Shinto shrine) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kannoni-in served as a {{ill|Prayer Temple|ja|祈願所|1=Kigan-sho|12=draft|12=draft}} for the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan|12=draft|12=draft}} during the medieval period. The temple is situated on a hill overlooking {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|12=draft|12=draft}}, providing a scenic view of the surrounding area.

The temple is featured in {{ill|Yasushi Inoue|ja|井上靖|12=draft|12=draft}}'s historical novel *{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (novel)|ja|風林火山 (小説)|12=draft|12=draft}}*, where it serves as the residence of {{ill|Yufu-hime|ja|諏訪御料人|12=draft|12=draft}}, a concubine of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen|12=draft|12=draft}} and the mother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼|12=draft|12=draft}}. revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Kannoni-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=岡谷市}} | location = Minato 4-chome 15-22, Okaya, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | latitude_deg = 36 | latitude_min = 2 | latitude_sec = 6.7 | longitude_deg = 138 | longitude_min = 4 | longitude_sec = 7.1 | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|12=draft|12=draft}} | deity = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音|12=draft|12=draft}} | founded = | founder = | full_name = Ryūkōzan Kannoni-in | former_affiliation = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (Shabō) | noteworthy = Historical ties to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人|12=draft|12=draft}} | map = Nagano Prefecture | map_relief = yes | map_width = 256 }} '''Kannoni-in''' (観音院), also known as '''Kosaka Kannoni-in''', is a Buddhist temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|12=draft|12=draft}} sect located in {{ill|Okaya|ja|岡谷市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is '''Ryūkōzan''' (龍光山), and its principal object of worship is the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== History == Originally a {{ill|Shabō|ja|社坊|12=draft|12=draft}} (a temple associated with a Shinto shrine) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kannoni-in served as a {{ill|Prayer Temple|ja|祈願所|1=Kigan-sho|12=draft|12=draft}} for the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan|12=draft|12=draft}} during the medieval period. The temple is situated on a hill overlooking {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|12=draft|12=draft}}, providing a scenic view of the surrounding area.
The temple was revived during the early Edo period under the patronage of {{ill|Suwa Tadatane|ja|諏訪忠恒|12=draft|12=draft}}, the lord of the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩|12=draft|12=draft}}. The current main hall was rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by {{ill|Suwa Tadatora|ja|諏訪忠虎|12=draft|12=draft}}.
The temple is featured in {{ill|Yasushi Inoue|ja|井上靖|12=draft|12=draft}}'s historical novel *{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (novel)|ja|風林火山 (小説)|12=draft|12=draft}}*, where it serves as the residence of {{ill|Yufu-hime|ja|諏訪御料人|12=draft|12=draft}}, a concubine of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen|12=draft|12=draft}} and the mother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼|12=draft|12=draft}}. The 2007 NHK {{ill|Taiga Drama|ja|大河ドラマ|12=draft|12=draft}} adaptation of *Fūrin Kazan* also portrays the temple.
In 1963 (Shōwa 38), a memorial tower dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin was erected on the temple grounds.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' - Rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by Suwa Tadatora. * '''Memorial Tower''' - Dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin in 1963.
== References == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines'' (1981). Shinano Mainichi Shimbun. * ''Exploring Shinshu's Ancient Temples: Tendai and Shingon Sects'' (1996). Kyodo Publishing.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kosaka Castle|ja|小坂城 (岡谷市)|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Minato Village|ja|湊村 (長野県)|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Okaya City Landmarks|ja|岡谷市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット|12=draft|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ Okaya City Tourism - Kosaka Kannoni-in]
{{buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|Nagano Prefecture}} {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Kannoni-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=岡谷市}} | location = Minato 4-chome 15-22, Okaya, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | latitude_deg = 36 | latitude_min = 2 | latitude_sec = 6.7 | longitude_deg = 138 | longitude_min = 4 | longitude_sec = 7.1 | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|12=draft|12=draft}} | deity = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音|12=draft|12=draft}} | founded = | founder = | full_name = Ryūkōzan Kannoni-in | former_affiliation = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (Shabō) | noteworthy = Historical ties to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人|12=draft|12=draft}} | map = Nagano Prefecture | map_relief = yes | map_width = 256 }} '''Kannoni-in''' (観音院), also known as '''Kosaka Kannoni-in''', is a Buddhist temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Chisan-ha|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|12=draft|12=draft}} sect located in {{ill|Okaya|ja|岡谷市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is '''Ryūkōzan''' (龍光山), and its principal object of worship is the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|ja|十一面観音|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== History == Originally a {{ill|Shabō|ja|社坊|12=draft|12=draft}} (a temple associated with a Shinto shrine) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kannoni-in served as a {{ill|Prayer Temple|ja|祈願所|1=Kigan-sho|12=draft|12=draft}} for the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan|12=draft|12=draft}} during the medieval period. The temple is situated on a hill overlooking {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|12=draft|12=draft}}, providing a scenic view of the surrounding area.
The temple was revived during the early Edo period under the patronage of {{ill|Suwa Tadatane|ja|諏訪忠恒|12=draft|12=draft}}, the lord of the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩|12=draft|12=draft}}. The current main hall was rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by {{ill|Suwa Tadatora|ja|諏訪忠虎|12=draft|12=draft}}.
The temple is featured in {{ill|Yasushi Inoue|ja|井上靖|12=draft|12=draft}}'s historical novel *{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (novel)|ja|風林火山 (小説)|12=draft|12=draft}}*, where it serves as the residence of {{ill|Yufu-hime|ja|諏訪御料人|12=draft|12=draft}}, a concubine of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen|12=draft|12=draft}} and the mother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼|12=draft|12=draft}}. The 2007 NHK {{ill|Taiga Drama|ja|大河ドラマ|12=draft|12=draft}} adaptation of *Fūrin Kazan* also portrays the temple.
In 1963 (Shōwa 38), a memorial tower dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin was erected on the temple grounds.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' - Rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by Suwa Tadatora. * '''Memorial Tower''' - Dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin in 1963.
== References == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines'' (1981). Shinano Mainichi Shimbun. * ''Exploring Shinshu's Ancient Temples: Tendai and Shingon Sects'' (1996). Kyodo Publishing.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kosaka Castle|ja|小坂城 (岡谷市)|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Minato Village|ja|湊村 (長野県)|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Okaya City Landmarks|ja|岡谷市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット|12=draft|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ Okaya City Tourism - Kosaka Kannoni-in]

revision.text:
{{otheruses|長野県岡谷市の寺院|その他の観音院|観音院 (曖昧さ回避)}} {{日本の寺院 |名称 = {{ruby|観音院|かんのんいん}} |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=岡谷市}} |所在地 = [[長野県]][[岡谷市]][[湊 (岡谷市)|湊]]4丁目15-22 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|2|6.7|N|138|4|7.1|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |山号 = 龍光山 |院号 = 観音院 |宗旨 = [[新義真言宗]] |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = 十一面観音 |創建年 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = 龍光山観音院 |別称 = 小坂観音院 |札所等 =[[伊那諏訪八十八霊場]]29番 |文化財 = }} '''観音院'''（かんのんいん）は、[[長野県]][[岡谷市]][[湊 (岡谷市)|湊]]四丁目にある[[真言宗智山派]]の寺院。山号は龍光山。本尊は[[十一面観音]]。一般的には「'''小坂観音院'''」の名で知られる。
== 由緒 == かつては[[諏訪大社]]の[[社坊]]であった。中世に[[諏訪氏]]の[[祈願所]]となった。[[諏訪湖]]を見渡せる眺望の良い高台にある。[[諏訪藩]]主[[諏訪忠恒]]を開基として中興された。本堂は正徳5年（1715年）[[諏訪忠虎]]によって再建された。
[[井上靖]]の著書「[[風林火山 (小説)|風林火山]]」では、[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]の娘[[諏訪御料人|由布姫]]（諏訪御料人）は、[[武田信玄]]の側室となって[[武田勝頼]]を生み、この観音院で暮らす設定になっている。井上靖の小説を原作とした[[平成]]19年（[[2007年]]）の[[日本放送協会|NHK]][[大河ドラマ]]「[[風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)|風林火山]]」でも観音院が登場する。
[[昭和]]38年（[[1963年]]）境内の高台に、諏訪御料人の供養塔が建てられた。
== 参考文献 == *『信州の文化シリーズ 寺と神社』1981年 [[信濃毎日新聞|信濃毎日新聞社]] *『探訪 信州の古寺 天台宗・真言宗』1996年 [[郷土出版社]]
== 関連項目 == * [[小坂城 (岡谷市)]] * [[湊村 (長野県)|湊村]] * [[岡谷市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット|岡谷市の名所・旧跡・観光スポット]] *[[岡谷市の歴史]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ 岡谷市役所（商業観光課）ホームページ「由布姫のふるさと 小坂観音院（龍光山観音院）」]
{{buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{shinto-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:観音院 (岡谷市)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|prefecture={{ill|Okaya|qid=Q838672}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.035194|N|138.068639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11630661]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11630661]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kannoni-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=岡谷市}} | location = Minato 4-chome 15-22, Okaya, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | latitude_deg = 36 | latitude_min = 2 | latitude_sec = 6.7 | longitude_deg = 138 | longitude_min = 4 | longitude_sec = 7.1 | sect = [[:en:Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha]] | deity = [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara]] | founded = | founder = | full_name = Ryūkōzan Kannoni-in | former_affiliation = [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] (Shabō) | noteworthy = Historical ties to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Goryōnin|qq=|1=Suwa Goryōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193299}} | map = Nagano Prefecture | map_relief = yes | map_width = 256 }} '''Kannoni-in''' (観音院), also known as '''Kosaka Kannoni-in''', is a Buddhist temple of the [[:en:Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha]] sect located in [[:en:Okaya, Nagano|Okaya]], Nagano Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is '''Ryūkōzan''' (龍光山), and its principal object of worship is the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara]].
== History == Originally a {{ill|Shabō|ja|社坊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shabō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神宮寺|1=jingū-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1074971}} (a temple associated with a Shinto shrine) of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], Kannoni-in served as a {{ill|Prayer Temple|ja|祈願所|1=Kigan-sho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prayer Temple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigan-sho|qq=}} for the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]] during the medieval period. The temple is situated on a hill overlooking [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]], providing a scenic view of the surrounding area.
The temple was revived during the early Edo period under the patronage of {{ill|Suwa Tadatane|ja|諏訪忠恒|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Tadatane|qq=|1=Suwa Tadatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631876}}, the lord of the [[:en:Suwa Domain|Suwa Domain]]. The current main hall was rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by {{ill|Suwa Tadatora|ja|諏訪忠虎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Tadatora|qq=|1=Suwa Tadatora|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631882}}.
The temple is featured in [[:en:Yasushi Inoue|Yasushi Inoue]]'s historical novel *{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (novel)|ja|風林火山 (小説)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fūrin Kazan (novel)|qq=|1=The Samurai Banner of Furin Kazan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665512}}*, where it serves as the residence of {{ill|Yufu-hime|ja|諏訪御料人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufu-hime|qq=|1=Suwa Goryōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193299}}, a concubine of [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the mother of [[:en:Takeda Katsuyori|Takeda Katsuyori]]. The 2007 NHK [[:en:Taiga drama|Taiga Drama]] adaptation of *Fūrin Kazan* also portrays the temple.
In 1963 (Shōwa 38), a memorial tower dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin was erected on the temple grounds.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' - Rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by Suwa Tadatora. * '''Memorial Tower''' - Dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin in 1963.
== References == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines'' (1981). Shinano Mainichi Shimbun. * ''Exploring Shinshu's Ancient Temples: Tendai and Shingon Sects'' (1996). Kyodo Publishing.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kosaka Castle|ja|小坂城 (岡谷市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosaka Castle|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Minato Village|ja|湊村 (長野県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato Village|qq=|1=Minato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563162}} * {{ill|Okaya City Landmarks|ja|岡谷市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okaya City Landmarks|qq=|1=Okaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838672}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:観音院 (岡谷市)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|prefecture={{ill|Okaya|WD=Q838672}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.035194|N|138.068639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11630661]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11630661]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kannoni-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=岡谷市}} | location = Minato 4-chome 15-22, Okaya, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | latitude_deg = 36 | latitude_min = 2 | latitude_sec = 6.7 | longitude_deg = 138 | longitude_min = 4 | longitude_sec = 7.1 | sect = [[:en:Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha]] | deity = [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara]] | founded = | founder = | full_name = Ryūkōzan Kannoni-in | former_affiliation = [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] (Shabō) | noteworthy = Historical ties to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Goryōnin|qq=|1=Suwa Goryōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193299}} | map = Nagano Prefecture | map_relief = yes | map_width = 256 }} '''Kannoni-in''' (観音院), also known as '''Kosaka Kannoni-in''', is a Buddhist temple of the [[:en:Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha]] sect located in [[:en:Okaya, Nagano|Okaya]], Nagano Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is '''Ryūkōzan''' (龍光山), and its principal object of worship is the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara]].
== History == Originally a {{ill|Shabō|ja|社坊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shabō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神宮寺|1=jingū-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1074971}} (a temple associated with a Shinto shrine) of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], Kannoni-in served as a {{ill|Prayer Temple|ja|祈願所|1=Kigan-sho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prayer Temple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigan-sho|qq=}} for the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]] during the medieval period. The temple is situated on a hill overlooking [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]], providing a scenic view of the surrounding area.
The temple was revived during the early Edo period under the patronage of {{ill|Suwa Tadatane|ja|諏訪忠恒|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Tadatane|qq=|1=Suwa Tadatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631876}}, the lord of the [[:en:Suwa Domain|Suwa Domain]]. The current main hall was rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by {{ill|Suwa Tadatora|ja|諏訪忠虎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Tadatora|qq=|1=Suwa Tadatora|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631882}}.
The temple is featured in [[:en:Yasushi Inoue|Yasushi Inoue]]'s historical novel *{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (novel)|ja|風林火山 (小説)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fūrin Kazan (novel)|qq=|1=The Samurai Banner of Furin Kazan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665512}}*, where it serves as the residence of {{ill|Yufu-hime|ja|諏訪御料人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufu-hime|qq=|1=Suwa Goryōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193299}}, a concubine of [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the mother of [[:en:Takeda Katsuyori|Takeda Katsuyori]]. The 2007 NHK [[:en:Taiga drama|Taiga Drama]] adaptation of *Fūrin Kazan* also portrays the temple.
In 1963 (Shōwa 38), a memorial tower dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin was erected on the temple grounds.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' - Rebuilt in 1715 (Shōtoku 5) by Suwa Tadatora. * '''Memorial Tower''' - Dedicated to Suwa Goryōnin in 1963.
== References == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines'' (1981). Shinano Mainichi Shimbun. * ''Exploring Shinshu's Ancient Temples: Tendai and Shingon Sects'' (1996). Kyodo Publishing.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kosaka Castle|ja|小坂城 (岡谷市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosaka Castle|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Minato Village|ja|湊村 (長野県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato Village|qq=|1=Minato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563162}} * {{ill|Okaya City Landmarks|ja|岡谷市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okaya City Landmarks|qq=|1=Okaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838672}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko-okaya.jp/tabitabi/jsp/furin/ Okaya City Tourism - Kosaka Kannoni-in]
